NKJV.1: Finally, brethren, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may run swiftly and be glorified, just as it is with you,
NKJV.2: and that we may be delivered from unreasonable and wicked men; for not all have faith.
NKJV.3: But the Lord is faithful, who will establish you and guard you from the evil one.
NKJV.4: And we have confidence in the Lord concerning you, both that you do and will do the things we command you.
NKJV.5: Now may the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God and into the patience of Christ.
NKJV.6: But we command you, brethren, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you withdraw from every brother who walks disorderly and not according to the tradition which he received from us.
NKJV.7: For you yourselves know how you ought to follow us, for we were not disorderly among you;
NKJV.8: nor did we eat anyone’s bread free of charge, but worked with labor and toil night and day, that we might not be a burden to any of you,
NKJV.9: not because we do not have authority, but to make ourselves an example of how you should follow us.
NKJV.10: For even when we were with you, we commanded you this: If anyone will not work, neither shall he eat.
NKJV.11: For we hear that there are some who walk among you in a disorderly manner, not working at all, but are busybodies.
NKJV.12: Now those who are such we command and exhort through our Lord Jesus Christ that they work in quietness and eat their own bread.
NKJV.13: But as for you, brethren, do not grow weary in doing good.
NKJV.14: And if anyone does not obey our word in this epistle, note that person and do not keep company with him, that he may be ashamed.
NKJV.15: Yet do not count him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.
NKJV.16: Now may the Lord of peace Himself give you peace always in every way. The Lord be with you all.
NKJV.17: The salutation of Paul with my own hand, which is a sign in every epistle; so I write.
NKJV.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

Amen<S>281</S>. <sup>The second<S>1208</S> epistle to<S>4314</S> the Thessalonians<S>2331</S> was written<S>1125</S> from<S>575</S> Athens<S>116</S>.</sup>

ESV.1: Finally, brothers,  pray for us, that the word of the Lord may speed ahead and be honored,  as happened among you,
ESV.2: and that we may be delivered from wicked and evil men. For not all have faith.
ESV.3: But the Lord is faithful. He will establish you and guard you against the evil one.
ESV.4: And we have confidence in the Lord about you, that you are doing and will do the things that we command.
ESV.5: May the Lord direct your hearts to the love of God and to the steadfastness of Christ.
ESV.6: Now we command you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you keep away from any brother who is walking in idleness and not in accord with the tradition that you received from us.
ESV.7: For you yourselves know how you ought to imitate us, because we were not idle when we were with you,
ESV.8: nor did we eat anyone's bread without paying for it, but with toil and labor we worked night and day, that we might not be a burden to any of you.
ESV.9: It was not because we do not have that right, but to give you in ourselves an example to imitate.
ESV.10: For even when we were with you, we would give you this command: If anyone is not willing to work, let him not eat.
ESV.11: For we hear that some among you walk in idleness, not busy at work, but busybodies.
ESV.12: Now such persons we command and encourage in the Lord Jesus Christ to do their work quietly and to earn their own living.
ESV.13: As for you, brothers, do not grow weary in doing good.
ESV.14: If anyone does not obey what we say in this letter, take note of that person, and have nothing to do with him, that he may be ashamed.
ESV.15: Do not regard him as an enemy, but warn him as a brother.
ESV.16: Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in every way. The Lord be with you all.
ESV.17: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand. This is the sign of genuineness in every letter of mine; it is the way I write.
ESV.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.

NASB.1: Finally, brethren, pray for us that the word of the Lord will spread rapidly and be glorified, just as [it did] also with you;
NASB.2: and that we will be rescued from perverse and evil men; for not all have faith.
NASB.3: But the Lord is faithful, and He will strengthen and protect you from the evil [one].
NASB.4: We have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that you are doing and will [continue to] do what we command.
NASB.5: May the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God and into the steadfastness of Christ.
NASB.6: Now we command you, brethren, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you keep away from every brother who leads an unruly life and not according to the tradition which you received from us.
NASB.7: For you yourselves know how you ought to follow our example, because we did not act in an undisciplined manner among you,
NASB.8: nor did we eat anyone's bread without paying for it, but with labor and hardship we [kept] working night and day so that we would not be a burden to any of you;
NASB.9: not because we do not have the right [to this], but in order to offer ourselves as a model for you, so that you would follow our example.
NASB.10: For even when we were with you, we used to give you this order: if anyone is not willing to work, then he is not to eat, either.
NASB.11: For we hear that some among you are leading an undisciplined life, doing no work at all, but acting like busybodies.
NASB.12: Now such persons we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ to work in quiet fashion and eat their own bread.
NASB.13: But as for you, brethren, do not grow weary of doing good.
NASB.14: If anyone does not obey our instruction in this letter, take special note of that person and do not associate with him, so that he will be put to shame.
NASB.15: [Yet] do not regard him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.
NASB.16: Now may the Lord of peace Himself continually grant you peace in every circumstance. The Lord be with you all!
NASB.17: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand, and this is a distinguishing mark in every letter; this is the way I write.
NASB.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.

NLT.1: Finally, dear brothers and sisters, we ask you to pray for us. Pray that the Lord’s message will spread rapidly and be honored wherever it goes, just as when it came to you.
NLT.2: Pray, too, that we will be rescued from wicked and evil people, for not everyone is a believer.
NLT.3: But the Lord is faithful; he will strengthen you and guard you from the evil one.
NLT.4: And we are confident in the Lord that you are doing and will continue to do the things we commanded you.
NLT.5: May the Lord lead your hearts into a full understanding and expression of the love of God and the patient endurance that comes from Christ.
NLT.6: And now, dear brothers and sisters, we give you this command in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: Stay away from all believers who live idle lives and don’t follow the tradition they received from us.
NLT.7: For you know that you ought to imitate us. We were not idle when we were with you.
NLT.8: We never accepted food from anyone without paying for it. We worked hard day and night so we would not be a burden to any of you.
NLT.9: We certainly had the right to ask you to feed us, but we wanted to give you an example to follow.
NLT.10: Even while we were with you, we gave you this command: “Those unwilling to work will not get to eat.”
NLT.11: Yet we hear that some of you are living idle lives, refusing to work and meddling in other people’s business.
NLT.12: We command such people and urge them in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ to settle down and work to earn their own living.
NLT.13: As for the rest of you, dear brothers and sisters, never get tired of doing good.
NLT.14: Take note of those who refuse to obey what we say in this letter. Stay away from them so they will be ashamed.
NLT.15: Don’t think of them as enemies, but warn them as you would a brother or sister.
NLT.16: Now may the Lord of peace himself give you his peace at all times and in every situation. The Lord be with you all.
NLT.17: HERE IS MY GREETING IN MY OWN HANDWRITING — PAUL. I DO THIS IN ALL MY LETTERS TO PROVE THEY ARE FROM ME.
NLT.18: May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.

WEB.1: Finally, brothers, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may spread rapidly and be glorified, even as also with you;
WEB.2: and that we may be delivered from unreasonable and evil men; for not all have faith.
WEB.3: But the Lord is faithful, who will establish you, and guard you from the evil one.
WEB.4: We have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that you both do and will do the things we command.
WEB.5: May the Lord direct your hearts into God’s love, and into the perseverance of Christ.
WEB.6: Now we command you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you withdraw yourselves from every brother who walks in rebellion, and not after the tradition which they received from us.
WEB.7: For you know how you ought to imitate us. For we didn’t behave ourselves rebelliously among you,
WEB.8: neither did we eat bread from anyone’s hand without paying for it, but in labor and travail worked night and day, that we might not burden any of you;
WEB.9: not because we don’t have the right, but to make ourselves an example to you, that you should imitate us.
WEB.10: For even when we were with you, we commanded you this: “If anyone will not work, don’t let him eat.”
WEB.11: For we hear of some who walk among you in rebellion, who don’t work at all, but are busybodies.
WEB.12: Now those who are that way, we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ, that with quietness they work, and eat their own bread.
WEB.13: But you, brothers, don’t be weary in doing well.
WEB.14: If any man doesn’t obey our word in this letter, note that man, that you have no company with him, to the end that he may be ashamed.
WEB.15: Don’t count him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.
WEB.16: Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in all ways. The Lord be with you all.
WEB.17: The greeting of me, Paul, with my own hand, which is the sign in every letter: this is how I write.
WEB.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

CSB17.1: In addition, brothers and sisters, pray for us that the word of the Lord may spread rapidly and be honored, just as it was with you, <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.2: and that we may be delivered <sup>ⓐ</sup> from wicked and evil people, for not all have faith. <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.3: But the Lord is faithful; he will strengthen and guard you from the evil one. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.4: We have confidence in the Lord about you, that you are doing and will continue to do what we command.
CSB17.5: May the Lord direct your hearts to God’s love and Christ’s endurance. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.6: Now we command you, brothers and sisters, in the name <sup>ⓓ</sup> of our Lord Jesus Christ, to keep away from every brother or sister <sup>ⓔ</sup> who is idle and does not live according to the tradition <sup>ⓕ</sup> received <sup>ⓖ</sup> from us.
CSB17.7: For you yourselves know how you should imitate <sup>ⓗ</sup> us: We were not idle among you;
CSB17.8: we did not eat <sup>ⓘ</sup> anyone’s food free of charge; <sup>ⓙ</sup> instead, we labored <sup>ⓚ</sup> and toiled, <sup>ⓛ</sup> working <sup>ⓜ</sup> night and day, so that we would not be a burden <sup>ⓝ</sup> to any of you.
CSB17.9: It is not that we don’t have the right <sup>ⓐ</sup> to support, but we did it to make ourselves an example to you so that you would imitate <sup>ⓑ</sup> us.
CSB17.10: In fact, when we were with you, this is what we commanded you: “If anyone isn’t willing to work, he should not eat.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.11: For we hear that there are some among you who are idle. They are not busy but busybodies. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.12: Now we command and exhort such people by the Lord Jesus Christ to work quietly and provide for themselves. <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.13: But as for you, brothers and sisters, do not grow weary <sup>ⓔ</sup> in doing good.
CSB17.14: If anyone does not obey our instruction in this letter, take note of that person; don’t associate with him, so that he may be ashamed.
CSB17.15: Yet don’t consider him as an enemy, but warn him as a brother.
CSB17.16: May the Lord of peace <sup>ⓕ</sup> himself give you peace always in every way. <sup>ⓖ</sup> The Lord be with all of you. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.17: I, Paul, am writing this greeting with my own hand, <sup>ⓘ</sup> which is an authenticating mark in every letter; this is how I write.
CSB17.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.

AMP.1: Finally, brothers and sisters, pray continually for us that the word of the Lord will spread rapidly and be honored [triumphantly celebrated and glorified], just as it was with you;
AMP.2: and [pray] that we will be rescued from perverse and evil men; for not everyone has the faith.
AMP.3: But the Lord is faithful, and He will strengthen you [setting you on a firm foundation] and will protect and guard you from the evil one.
AMP.4: We have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that you are doing and will continue to do the things [which] we command.
AMP.5: May the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God and into the steadfastness and patience of Christ.
AMP.6: Now we command you, believers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and by His authority, that you withdraw and keep away from every brother or sister who leads an undisciplined life and does not live in accordance with the tradition and teaching that you have received from us.
AMP.7: For you yourselves know how you ought to follow our example, because we did not act in an undisciplined or inappropriate manner when we were with you [we were never idle or lazy, nor did we avoid our duties],
AMP.8: nor did we eat anyone's bread without paying for it, but with labor and hardship we worked night and day [to pay our own way] so that we would not be a financial burden on any of you [for our support];
AMP.9: not because we do not have a right to such support, but [we provided our own financial support] to offer ourselves as a model for you, so that you would follow our example.
AMP.10: For even while we were with you, we used to give you this order: if anyone is not willing to work, then he is not to eat, either.
AMP.11: Indeed, we hear that some among you are leading an undisciplined and inappropriate life, doing no work at all, but acting like busybodies [meddling in other people's business].
AMP.12: Now such people we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ to settle down and work quietly and earn their own food and other necessities [supporting themselves instead of depending on the hospitality of others].
AMP.13: And as for [the rest of] you, believers, do not grow tired or lose heart in doing good [but continue doing what is right without weakening].
AMP.14: Now if anyone [in the church] does not obey what we say in this letter, take special note of that person and do not associate with him, so that he will be ashamed and repent.
AMP.15: Do not regard him as an enemy, but keep admonishing him as a [believing] brother.
AMP.16: Now may the Lord of peace Himself grant you His peace at all times and in every way [that peace and spiritual well-being that comes to those who walk with Him, regardless of life's circumstances]. The Lord be with you all.
AMP.17: I, Paul, write you this final greeting with my own hand. This is the distinguishing mark in every letter [of mine, that shows it is genuine]. It is the way I write [my handwriting and signature].
AMP.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.

MSG.1: One more thing, friends: Pray for us. Pray that the Master's Word will simply take off and race through the country to a groundswell of response, just as it did among you.
MSG.2: And pray that we'll be rescued from these scoundrels who are trying to do us in. I'm finding that not all "believers" are believers.
MSG.3: But the Master never lets us down. He'll stick by you and protect you from evil.
MSG.4: Because of the Master, we have great confidence in you. We know you're doing everything we told you and will continue doing it.
MSG.5: May the Master take you by the hand and lead you along the path of God's love and Christ's endurance.
MSG.6: Our orders—backed up by the Master, Jesus—are to refuse to have anything to do with those among you who are lazy and refuse to work the way we taught you. Don't permit them to freeload on the rest.
MSG.7: We showed you how to pull your weight when we were with you, so get on with it.
MSG.8: We didn't sit around on our hands expecting others to take care of us. In fact, we worked our fingers to the bone, up half the night moonlighting so you wouldn't be burdened with taking care of us.
MSG.9: And it wasn't because we didn't have a right to your support; we did. We simply wanted to provide an example of diligence, hoping it would prove contagious.
MSG.10: Don't you remember the rule we had when we lived with you? "If you don't work, you don't eat."
MSG.11: And now we're getting reports that a bunch of lazy good-for-nothings are taking advantage of you.
MSG.12: This must not be tolerated. We command them to get to work immediately—no excuses, no arguments—and earn their own keep.
MSG.13: Friends, don't slack off in doing your duty.
MSG.14: If anyone refuses to obey our clear command written in this letter, don't let him get by with it. Point out such a person and refuse to subsidize his freeloading. Maybe then he'll think twice.
MSG.15: But don't treat him as an enemy. Sit him down and talk about the problem as someone who cares.
MSG.16: May the Master of Peace himself give you the gift of getting along with each other at all times, in all ways. May the Master be truly among you!
MSG.17: I, Paul, bid you good-bye in my own handwriting. I do this in all my letters, so examine my signature as proof that the letter is genuine.
MSG.18: The incredible grace of our Master, Jesus Christ, be with all of you!

NIV.1:
Request for Prayer
Finally, brothers, pray for us that the message of the Lord may spread rapidly and be honored, just as it was with you.
NIV.2: And pray that we may be delivered from wicked and evil men, for not everyone has faith.
NIV.3: But the Lord is faithful, and he will strengthen and protect you from the evil one.
NIV.4: We have confidence in the Lord that you are doing and will continue to do the things we command.
NIV.5: May the Lord direct your hearts into God’s love and Christ’s perseverance.
NIV.6:
Warning Against Idleness
In the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, we command you, brothers, to keep away from every brother who is idle and does not live according to the teaching you received from us.
NIV.7: For you yourselves know how you ought to follow our example. We were not idle when we were with you,
NIV.8: nor did we eat anyone’s food without paying for it. On the contrary, we worked night and day, laboring and toiling so that we would not be a burden to any of you.
NIV.9: We did this, not because we do not have the right to such help, but in order to make ourselves a model for you to follow.
NIV.10: For even when we were with you, we gave you this rule: “If a man will not work, he shall not eat.”
NIV.11: We hear that some among you are idle. They are not busy; they are busybodies.
NIV.12: Such people we command and urge in the Lord Jesus Christ to settle down and earn the bread they eat.
NIV.13: And as for you, brothers, never tire of doing what is right.
NIV.14: If anyone does not obey our instruction in this letter, take special note of him. Do not associate with him, in order that he may feel ashamed.
NIV.15: Yet do not regard him as an enemy, but warn him as a brother.
NIV.16:
Final Greetings
Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times and in every way. The Lord be with all of you.
NIV.17: I, Paul, write this greeting in my own hand, which is the distinguishing mark in all my letters. This is how I write.
NIV.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.

NRSVCE.1: Finally, brothers and sisters, pray for us, so that the word of the Lord may spread rapidly and be glorified everywhere, just as it is among you,
NRSVCE.2: and that we may be rescued from wicked and evil people; for not all have faith.
NRSVCE.3: But the Lord is faithful; he will strengthen you and guard you from the evil one.
NRSVCE.4: And we have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that you are doing and will go on doing the things that we command.
NRSVCE.5: May the Lord direct your hearts to the love of God and to the steadfastness of Christ.
NRSVCE.6: Now we command you, beloved, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to keep away from believers who are living in idleness and not according to the tradition that they received from us.
NRSVCE.7: For you yourselves know how you ought to imitate us; we were not idle when we were with you,
NRSVCE.8: and we did not eat anyone’s bread without paying for it; but with toil and labor we worked night and day, so that we might not burden any of you.
NRSVCE.9: This was not because we do not have that right, but in order to give you an example to imitate.
NRSVCE.10: For even when we were with you, we gave you this command: Anyone unwilling to work should not eat.
NRSVCE.11: For we hear that some of you are living in idleness, mere busybodies, not doing any work.
NRSVCE.12: Now such persons we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ to do their work quietly and to earn their own living.
NRSVCE.13: Brothers and sisters, do not be weary in doing what is right.
NRSVCE.14: Take note of those who do not obey what we say in this letter; have nothing to do with them, so that they may be ashamed.
NRSVCE.15: Do not regard them as enemies, but warn them as believers.
NRSVCE.16: Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in all ways. The Lord be with all of you.
NRSVCE.17: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand. This is the mark in every letter of mine; it is the way I write.
NRSVCE.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.

NET.1: Finally, pray for us, brothers and sisters, that the Lord’s message may spread quickly and be honored as in fact it was among you,
NET.2: and that we may be delivered from perverse and evil people. For not all have faith.
NET.3: But the Lord is faithful, and he will strengthen you and protect you from the evil one.
NET.4: And we are confident about you in the Lord that you are both doing — and will do — what we are commanding.
NET.5: Now may the Lord direct your hearts toward the love of God and the endurance of Christ.
NET.6: But we command you, brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to keep away from any brother who lives an undisciplined life and not according to the tradition they received from us.
NET.7: For you know yourselves how you must imitate us, because we did not behave without discipline among you,
NET.8: and we did not eat anyone’s food without paying. Instead, in toil and drudgery we worked night and day in order not to burden any of you.
NET.9: It was not because we do not have that right, but to give ourselves as an example for you to imitate.
NET.10: For even when we were with you, we used to give you this command: “If anyone is not willing to work, neither should he eat.”
NET.11: For we hear that some among you are living an undisciplined life, not doing their own work but meddling in the work of others.
NET.12: Now such people we command and urge in the Lord Jesus Christ to work quietly and so provide their own food to eat.
NET.13: But you, brothers and sisters, do not grow weary in doing what is right.
NET.14: But if anyone does not obey our message through this letter, take note of him and do not associate closely with him, so that he may be ashamed.
NET.15: Yet do not regard him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.
NET.16: Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times and in every way. The Lord be with you all.
NET.17: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand, which is how I write in every letter.
NET.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
